title: vigna vexillata var. sepiaria
intro: Vigna vexillata var. sepiaria, also known as Bushy Vetch or African Horsegram, is a perennial legume native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood sugar levels, managing blood pressure, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, Vigna vexillata var. sepiaria is often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
